segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered,
Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty that you are righteous? Or does it benefit him that you make your ways perfect?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is it for your piety that he reproves you, that he enters with you into judgment?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Isn’t your wickedness great? Neither is there any end to your iniquities.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For you have taken pledges from your brother for nothing, and stripped the naked of their clothing.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You haven’t given water to the weary to drink, and you have withheld bread from the hungry.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: But as for the mighty man, he had the earth. The honorable man, he lived in it.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You have sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Therefore snares are around you. Sudden fear troubles you,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: or darkness, so that you can not see, and floods of waters cover you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: “Isn’t God in the heights of heaven? See the height of the stars, how high they are!
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You say, ‘What does God know? Can he judge through the thick darkness?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Thick clouds are a covering to him, so that he doesn’t see. He walks on the vault of the sky.’
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Will you keep the old way, which wicked men have trodden,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: who were snatched away before their time, whose foundation was poured out as a stream,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: who said to God, ‘Depart from us!’and, ‘What can the Almighty do for us?’
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Yet he filled their houses with good things, but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The righteous see it, and are glad. The innocent ridicule them,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: saying, ‘Surely those who rose up against us are cut off. The fire has consumed their remnant.’
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: “Acquaint yourself with him now, and be at peace. By it, good will come to you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Please receive instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in your heart.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: If you return to the Almighty, you will be built up, if you put away unrighteousness far from your tents.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Lay your treasure in the dust, the gold of Ophir among the stones of the brooks.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The Almighty will be your treasure, and precious silver to you.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and will lift up your face to God.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You will make your prayer to him, and he will hear you. You will pay your vows.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: You will also decree a thing, and it will be established to you. Light will shine on your ways.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: When they cast down, you will say, ‘be lifted up.’He will save the humble person.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He will even deliver him who is not innocent. Yes, he will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz
